sections_cited: section 47 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special STRA 143 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.1672/2025. 2. For orders on office objection No.1. 3. For orders on CMA No.1673/2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 11.12.2025 On the last date following order was passed: “20.11.2025 Mr. Mansoor Shaikh, advocate for applicant. Per learned counsel, reference is delayed by 530 days. Time is given to assist. To come up after two weeks.” Today applicant is unrepresented without intimation or justification. In view hereof, it appears that the applicant department is not interested in present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 47 of the Sales Tax Act, 1990. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
